# UC-22.50.39 · GLBA §314.4(d)(2) — Continuous monitoring: security-event detection and response-readiness evidence

## Description

Tracks continuous monitoring effectiveness for GLBA §314.4(d)(2) by joining security detections to their corresponding incident tickets, measuring actual detection-to-response and detection-to-resolution metrics. Replaces the prior version that calculated metrics without joining detections to tickets.

## Value

§314.4(d)(2) requires continuous monitoring to detect actual and attempted attacks or intrusions. Tracking detection-to-response ratios and response times demonstrates the monitoring programme is effective.

## Implementation

(1) Correlate ES notables with incident-response tickets; (2) schedule daily; (3) escalate low detection-to-response ratios and slow response times to SOC management. Indexes required: `index=incident_response`, `index=notable`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ir_tickets`, `sourcetype=stash`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Splunk ES notable events, IDS/IPS alerts, SIEM correlation rules, incident-response ticket logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Correlate ES notables with incident-response tickets; (2) schedule daily; (3) escalate low detection-to-response ratios and slow response times to SOC management.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=notable sourcetype=stash earliest=-30d
| eval detected_time=_time
| lookup ir_ticket_correlation.csv rule_id OUTPUT ticket_id, ticket_created, ticket_resolved
| eval has_ticket=if(isnotnull(ticket_id), 1, 0)
| eval response_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_created), round((ticket_created - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| eval resolution_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_resolved), round((ticket_resolved - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| stats count AS detections sum(has_ticket) AS with_tickets avg(response_hrs) AS avg_response_hrs avg(resolution_hrs) AS avg_resolution_hrs BY security_domain
| eval ticket_coverage_pct=round(100*with_tickets/detections, 1)
| where ticket_coverage_pct<100 OR avg_response_hrs>4
| table security_domain, detections, with_tickets, ticket_coverage_pct, avg_response_hrs, avg_resolution_hrs
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.

## SPL

```spl
index=notable sourcetype=stash earliest=-30d
| eval detected_time=_time
| lookup ir_ticket_correlation.csv rule_id OUTPUT ticket_id, ticket_created, ticket_resolved
| eval has_ticket=if(isnotnull(ticket_id), 1, 0)
| eval response_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_created), round((ticket_created - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| eval resolution_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_resolved), round((ticket_resolved - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| stats count AS detections sum(has_ticket) AS with_tickets avg(response_hrs) AS avg_response_hrs avg(resolution_hrs) AS avg_resolution_hrs BY security_domain
| eval ticket_coverage_pct=round(100*with_tickets/detections, 1)
| where ticket_coverage_pct<100 OR avg_response_hrs>4
| table security_domain, detections, with_tickets, ticket_coverage_pct, avg_response_hrs, avg_resolution_hrs
```

## Visualization

Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `active_rules`, `avg_response_hrs`, `detected`, `detection_to_response_pct`, `detections`, `ir_tickets`.

## References

- [GLBA Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314)](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-16/chapter-I/subchapter-C/part-314)
